title: Renewable Energy
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text:
The transition to renewable energy sources has become a global (0). as countries work to reduce their carbon emissions. Solar and wind power have seen remarkable growth in recent years, with costs (1). significantly due to technological advances. Many nations have set ambitious targets to (2). away from fossil fuels entirely within the coming decades.

However, the shift presents considerable challenges. One major (3). is that renewable sources depend on weather conditions, making energy supply less predictable. To (4). this problem, scientists are developing better battery storage systems. These innovations will help (5). a steady electricity supply even when the sun is not shining or the wind is not blowing.

Governments are also (6). incentives to encourage both businesses and households to adopt clean energy. Tax benefits and subsidies have (7). effective in promoting solar panel installation. As technology continues to improve and prices fall, experts predict that renewable energy will eventually (8). over from traditional power sources completely.

example.explanation: 'Priority' is the word that best expresses something considered urgent and of primary importance.

questions.explanation: 'Dropping' is commonly used to describe the fall in prices or costs.
'Move away from' es el phrasal verb correcto para alejarse de algo gradualmente.
'Drawback' means disadvantage or downside, appropriate in technical contexts.
'Address a problem' is the formal collocation for dealing with or tackling a problem.
'Ensure' significa asegurar o garantizar que algo suceda.
'Provide incentives' is the correct collocation for offering incentives.
'Take over' es el phrasal verb que significa asumir el control o reemplazar.
